evals.name: full pass — all structural and behavioral checks
evals.prompt:
I have a project and its plan manifest. The plan-manifest.json describes what was supposed to be implemented. Please verify that the implementation matches the plan.

Project root: attached files (sample-project/)
Plan manifest: evals/files/sample-project/plan-manifest.json

Perform a full verification:
1. Run all structural checks (file existence, exports, types, functions)
2. Run all behavioral checks (error handling, input validation)
3. Calculate the compliance score
4. Produce a verification report with pass/fail/skipped breakdown

IMPORTANT: Your report MUST include a Detailed Checkpoint Results table listing EVERY checkpoint by its ID (CP-001, CP-002, ..., CP-011) with its verdict (Pass/Fail/Skipped) and reason. Do NOT report only aggregate counts.

Report the overall compliance score and whether it PASSES.
evals.expected_output: The verifier produces a report showing 100% compliance — all 11 checkpoints pass (8 structural + 3 behavioral). The verdict should be PASS. The Detailed Checkpoint Results table lists each checkpoint by ID.
assertions: The output reports a compliance score of 100%.
The output shows all 11 checkpoints as passed (or all structural and behavioral checks passed).
The verdict is PASS or ✅ PASS or Full Compliance.
The output includes a Detailed Checkpoint Results table listing individual checkpoint IDs with their verdicts.
The output mentions CP-001 (or CP-001 through CP-008) as structural checks that passed.
The output mentions CP-009 (or CP-009 through CP-011) as behavioral checks that passed.
The report format includes a Results Summary table with Category, Total, Passed, Failed, Skipped columns.

evals.name: partial fail — missing input validation in some services
evals.prompt:
Verify the implementation against the plan manifest for the inventory and payment services project.

Project root: attached files (sample-with-issues/)
Plan manifest: evals/files/sample-with-issues/plan-manifest.json

The plan specifies that:
- InventoryService.addStock should validate quantity > 0
- PaymentService.processPayment should validate amount > 0
- InventoryService.removeStock should handle insufficient stock errors
- PaymentService.refundPayment should handle missing payment errors

Run full structural and behavioral verification. Report compliance score and identify any failures.

IMPORTANT: Your report MUST include a Detailed Checkpoint Results table listing EVERY checkpoint by its ID (CP-001 through CP-009) with its verdict (Pass/Fail/Skipped) and reason.
evals.expected_output: The verifier identifies that CP-006 (addStock input validation) and CP-007 (processPayment input validation) FAIL because those methods do not validate their inputs. CP-008 (removeStock) and CP-009 (refundPayment) PASS because they use if-guard-throw patterns for error handling. The compliance score should be ~77.8% (7/9 = 77.8%).
assertions: The output shows a compliance score less than 100% (partial or low compliance).
The output reports CP-006 (addStock validates input) as FAILED because the method has no input validation.
The output reports CP-007 (processPayment validates input) as FAILED because the method has no input validation.
The output reports CP-001 through CP-005 as PASSED (structural checks).
The output reports that CP-008 (removeStock handles error) or the error handling check for removeStock is PASSED.
The output reports that CP-009 (refundPayment handles error) or the error handling check for refundPayment is PASSED.
The verdict is PARTIAL or FAIL (not PASS since score < 100%).
The output includes a Detailed Checkpoint Results table listing all checkpoint IDs (CP-001 through CP-009).

evals.name: missing file — file referenced in manifest does not exist
evals.prompt:
Verify this project against its plan manifest. The project is missing some files.

Plan manifest: I'll describe it inline.

The plan manifest has these checkpoints:
- CP-001: structural, fileExists, target: src/existing-service.ts ✓ (file exists)
- CP-002: structural, fileExists, target: src/missing-service.ts ✗ (file does NOT exist)
- CP-003: structural, exportExists (exportName: "ExistingService"), target: src/existing-service.ts, dependsOn: [CP-001]
- CP-004: structural, exportExists (exportName: "MissingService"), target: src/missing-service.ts, dependsOn: [CP-002]

The project has only this file:

src/existing-service.ts:
```typescript
export class ExistingService {
  doSomething(): string {
    return "done";
  }
}
```

src/missing-service.ts does NOT exist.

Run the verification. Report which checkpoints pass, fail, or are skipped due to dependency failures.
evals.expected_output:
The verifier reports:
- CP-001: PASS (file exists)
- CP-002: FAIL (file not found)
- CP-003: PASS (export exists, and CP-001 passed)
- CP-004: SKIPPED (depends on CP-002 which failed)
Compliance score: 2/3 = 66.7% (CP-004 is skipped, excluded from denominator)
assertions: The output reports CP-002 as FAILED because the file src/missing-service.ts does not exist.
The output reports CP-004 as SKIPPED because it depends on CP-002 which failed.
The compliance score is calculated as Passed / (Total - Skipped), resulting in approximately 66.7%.
The output explains that CP-004 is skipped due to dependency failure (not counted as failed).
The verdict indicates LOW COMPLIANCE or PARTIAL.
